author: potuz
content: Hello pandas, what would be an effective path to test attestation deadline at 2 seconds instead of 3?

author: barnabasbusa
content: change the % in the config

author: barnabasbusa
content: all of these are exposed under network_params in kurtosis

author: barnabasbusa
content: you will have to change everything else too, as everything is % point now

author: barnabasbusa
content: dependent on what you want for the other values, adjust `attestation_due_bps_gloas: 1666` and increase the aggregate or whatever else

author: tersec
content: what is the goal here?

author: tersec
content: in a non-6s context

author: potuz
content:
Testing in these single server devnets it's a first step but only to find out if we're screwed from the start.

<@654267572107083777> the broadcast time up to the attestation deadline becomes effectively useless time of the slot in ePBS. By lowering it we can lower the payload timelines check. Guaranteeing the same execution/proving time without removing the Payload broadcast time. This also allows us to squeeze more from timing games and also reduces the UX deficit of delaying tx inclusion for the time between CL block availability and EL block availability

author: barnabasbusa
content: Do we wanna touch changing these times in gloas? I'd actually hold off and just leave everything as is, and iterate further on slot restructuring in the following fork. I'm a bit afraid that we won't have too much gain unless we shorten the slot time

author: parithosh
content: i think potuz isnt proposing changing the slot time but just when the attestation deadline occurs at

author: parithosh
content: to reduce dead time in the slot

author: potuz
content: I think this is reasonable, specially for PTC deadlines like dual deadlines. But attestation deadline is a little trickier cause it will have to be a hard fork to change it, so I would rather pick the right one now. Specially since the block size will not change at all with gas limit

author: potuz
content: So it makes sense to get it right.

author: tersec
content: That's consistent with this though,  no? The counter-proposal here is not to do this in Gloas, but to do this as part of a broader look at slot restructuring in the next hardfork

author: tersec
content: There are a lot of things it "makes sense to get" right, but there's a limited amount it makes sense to change each hardfork

author: barnabasbusa
content: yeah I'd just stick to the current values, even if we have a ton of deadtime

author: barnabasbusa
content: and figure out the right times in heze

author: potuz
content: Problem is that at 3 seconds deadline and 6 second PTC payload deadline, it gives only 2 seconds for Payload broadcast. This seems already tight for 200M gas. So leaving the attestation deadline at 3" will force us to move PTC dual deadline to after 6 seconds which will effectively cap gas limit to prevent the block from executing into the next slot (something we can't really handle at forkchoice).

author: potuz
content: In other words, if we keep numbers as exactly speced right now, we cannot allow a gas limit that takes 3 seconds to execute

author: potuz
content: Thus we can't handle 200M as promised

author: nflaig
content: heze will make it more complicated with FOCIL, so I think we should figure out good values now

author: tersec
content:
> This seems already tight for 200M gas.

200M gas is an aspirational goal inasmuch as 100M gas was an aspirational goal last year. It's nice if it happens, yeah. And it can/should guide some decisions. But it's not a commitment in the same sense as a concrete EIP

author: tersec
content: This is not to say anything against it, but the process for deciding 200M was ... not very rigorous. And it's suspiciously a big round number

author: tersec
content: There was no 200M promise, at least no more than there was a 100M promise. That did not happen, and that's ok

author: tersec
content: I'm being a bit directly here because I want to avoid the semantic drift from "By Friday, the three threads converged on the headline number for the week: a credible 200M post-Glamsterdam gas limit floor." to a "promise"

author: potuz
content: here I disagree, we can easily do much more now, we have a lot of slack, we don't do it just because we aren't enforcing better timings. I don't want to stress the nodes, I just want to measure what we can do comfortably

author: potuz
content: So as an example, we can simply go from 3 seconds to 6 seconds by just moving the PTC to dual deadlines, no hard fork, no nothing

author: tersec
content: "aspirational" doesn't mean "unachievable"

author: tersec
content: I'm trying to be fairly precise. It is and always was a handwavy number. _Literally_ handwavy

author: tersec
content: Yes, it comes from somewhere, I know

author: potuz
content: I think we are more or less on the same boat, to have something less handwavy we need benches, and that's what I am asking for

author: tersec
content: Ok, the core/only point, and I really don't want to spam too much more, is the idea that 200M is a "promise". That's really all these last few messages of mine are addressing. It simply is not

author: tersec
content: It's "credible"

author: tersec
content: This isn't to disagree with scaling as some general idea, it's to say 200M isn't a magic threshold

author: tersec
content: broadly, sure

author: potuz
content: that's fine, I'm only using these numbers as "what we can do now", today with 3 seconds we can't do much more than 60M, if we merge https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5212 then we get much more (whatever benches give for 6 seconds execution), and if we move the attestation deadline to 2 seconds then we get also more stability in the happy case.

author: tersec
content: anyway, I agree with you about testing this
